title: Angular Basic — Paper 1
description: Foundational Angular concepts: components, data binding, directives, component communication, lifecycle hooks, services, modules, template-driven forms, pipes, and CLI.

questions.text: Which decorator is used to define an Angular component?
In Angular, what does interpolation syntax look like?
Which built-in directive conditionally adds or removes an element from the DOM?
Which decorator marks a component property so a parent component can pass data into it?
Which lifecycle hook is called once, after the component's first ngOnChanges, to perform component initialization?
What does @Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' }) mean for an Angular service?

options.text: The service is provided only within a specific module
The service creates a new instance for every component that injects it
The service is registered as a singleton in the root injector
The service can only be injected into root components

questions.text: In a traditional NgModule-based application, which property of @NgModule lists the components, directives, and pipes that belong to the module?
Which directive must be applied to a form element to enable Angular template-driven form functionality?
Which built-in Angular pipe transforms a string to all uppercase letters?
Which Angular CLI command creates a new Angular project?
What is the purpose of the 'selector' property in the @Component decorator?

options.text: It defines the CSS file path for the component
It specifies the HTML tag used to render the component
It sets the component's routing path
It declares which module the component belongs to

questions.text: What syntax is used for property binding in Angular templates?
Which directive is used to iterate over a collection in Angular templates?
Which decorator is used to emit events from a child component to its parent?
Which lifecycle hook is called every time an @Input() property value changes in a component?
Which Angular decorator marks a class as injectable as a dependency?
Which property of @NgModule is used to make components, directives, or pipes available to other modules?
Which module must be imported to use ngModel for two-way data binding in template-driven forms?
Which built-in pipe is used to format a number as currency in Angular?
Which Angular CLI command is used to run a development server and serve the application locally?
In Angular 14+, what is a standalone component?

options.text: A component that can only be used once in the application
A component declared outside any class
A component that does not require being declared in an NgModule
A component with no external dependencies

questions.text: What is the syntax for event binding in Angular templates?
What does the ngClass directive do in Angular?

options.text: It generates a new CSS class at runtime
It dynamically adds or removes CSS classes on an element
It applies inline styles to an element
It scopes CSS to the component

questions.text: What is @ViewChild used for in Angular?

options.text: To pass data from parent to child component
To get a reference to a child component, directive, or DOM element in the template
To emit events from a child component

questions.text: Which lifecycle hook should you use to clean up subscriptions and resources when a component is destroyed?
How can you inject a service into a component in Angular without using the constructor?

options.text: Using the inject() function in the component body

questions.text: Which property of @NgModule lists other modules whose exported declarations are available within the current module?
In template-driven forms, which CSS class is applied to an input when its value has been modified by the user?
Which Angular pipe subscribes to an Observable or Promise and returns the latest emitted value directly in the template?
What file in an Angular project is the primary entry point that bootstraps the application?
